title: Thinking about democracy in turbulent times [Book Review]
author: Royer

opening_move.description: The review opens by endorsing Keane's diagnosis of 'professional thoughtlessness' in democratic theory, but immediately deflates it: Keane himself fails to deliver on his promise of 'radically unorthodox' thinking.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Democratic theory needs radical rethinking — but this book does not provide it, making the review a diagnostic of the field's unmet demand

theoretical_positioning.description: The review is a critical assessment of Keane's lectures — sympathetic to the ambition, critical of the execution; the reviewer argues the book fails to transcend the 'professional thoughtlessness' it diagnoses.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: close reading and critical evaluation of Keane's argument
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review genre with strong critical stance.

case_type.case_name: Keane, Thinking About Democracy in Turbulent Times (2025)

confidence_notes: Book review genre. Notable as a genuinely critical (negative) review rather than an appreciative one — the reviewer argues the book fails to deliver on its own ambition.
